shortDescription: Surgical relocation of your own hair follicles to thinning or balding areas of the scalp

sections.overview:
A hair transplant moves healthy follicles from a donor area, usually the back and sides of the scalp, to areas where hair has thinned or stopped growing. Patients choose Korea because its clinics combine high-volume aesthetic expertise with internationally accredited facilities and transparent pricing.

The procedure treats pattern hair loss, a receding hairline, thinning crowns, and scarring from earlier surgery or injury. Because the relocated follicles are taken from areas that are genetically resistant to the hormone driving most hair loss, transplanted hair generally continues to grow in its new position for life. The result looks natural when the angle, density, and hairline design follow the way your hair grew before.

Two main techniques are used. Follicular unit extraction removes individual follicular units one at a time, leaving tiny dot-shaped marks rather than a line. Follicular unit transplantation, sometimes called the strip method, removes a thin band of scalp from the donor area and divides it into grafts under magnification. Both are performed under local anesthesia, so you stay awake but feel no pain during the session.

A single session commonly transplants somewhere in the range of 2,000 to 4,000 follicles, depending on the size of the area being treated and the density of your donor zone. Larger restorations may be planned across more than one session. The transplanted hairs typically shed within the first weeks, which is normal and expected, before new growth begins. Honest planning matters here: a transplant redistributes the hair you have rather than creating new hair, so realistic goals are set during consultation before any surgery is scheduled.
sections.candidacy:
You may be a candidate if you have stable, well-defined hair loss and a healthy donor area at the back and sides of the scalp with enough density to spare. The procedure suits men with pattern baldness, women with localised thinning, and people wanting to restore a hairline or cover scarring from prior surgery or injury. Good general health and the absence of active scalp infection or uncontrolled skin disease are important.

A transplant is less suitable if hair loss is still rapidly progressing, because newly transplanted hair will not stop the surrounding native hair from continuing to thin, which can leave gaps over time. In these cases a clinic may suggest stabilising the loss first, or planning conservatively, before committing to surgery. People with very limited donor density may not have enough follicles to achieve the coverage they hope for, and this is discussed openly during consultation.

During assessment the clinic reviews your scalp, the pattern and likely future course of your hair loss, and your expectations. Photographs and a donor-area evaluation help confirm whether the result you want is achievable. If you take medication that affects bleeding or wound healing, share this in advance. Honest expectation-setting is part of candidacy: the aim is a natural, lasting improvement, not a guaranteed return to teenage density.
sections.procedure_steps:
On the day, the donor and recipient areas are cleaned and marked, and the new hairline is designed with you so it suits your face and age. Local anesthesia numbs the scalp, and most people find the session comfortable once it takes effect.

If follicular unit extraction is used, individual follicular units are removed one by one from the donor area using a fine punch, leaving scattered dot-shaped marks rather than a linear scar. This approach lets you keep hair short afterwards and suits people who prefer no visible line. If follicular unit transplantation, or the strip method, is chosen, a narrow band of scalp is removed from the donor area and closed with sutures, then divided into individual grafts under magnification. The strip method can yield a large number of grafts efficiently in one session and leaves a fine linear mark that hair usually covers.

With either technique, tiny incisions are made in the recipient area at the correct angle and direction to mimic natural growth, and the prepared follicles are placed one at a time. Careful attention to angle, depth, and density is what makes the result look natural rather than uniform or pluggy. A session commonly involves 2,000 to 4,000 follicles and can take several hours depending on the count. You remain awake throughout and can usually take short breaks. Once placement is complete, the areas are cleaned and aftercare instructions are given before you leave.
sections.recovery_timeline:
Most patients stay around five days, which covers the procedure, an early review, and the first wash before you fly home. Immediately afterward the scalp may feel tight and look red, with small crusts forming around each graft. These crusts typically flake away over the first week to ten days as the grafts settle.

Mild swelling of the forehead can appear in the first few days and then subsides on its own. Any donor-area soreness, and a fine line if the strip method was used, eases over the same period. The clinic usually advises gentle, careful washing from the early days and explains how to protect the new grafts from rubbing, scratching, or direct sun. Strenuous exercise, swimming, and saunas are generally avoided for the first few weeks.

It is normal and expected for the transplanted hairs to shed within the first two to six weeks. This shock loss can be discouraging if you are not prepared for it, so it helps to know it is a stage on the way to new growth rather than a failure. New hair usually begins to emerge after a few months, thickens gradually, and continues to mature over roughly a year, with the fullest result visible toward the end of that period.

Flying is generally fine once the early review confirms the grafts are stable, often around the end of the stay. Patients book a return consultation or send progress photos so the clinic can follow growth over the months that follow.
sections.cost_breakdown:
Hair transplant pricing in Korea commonly ranges from about $3,500 to $12,000. The main driver of cost is the number of follicles transplanted, since larger areas need more grafts and longer sessions. The technique also matters: follicular unit extraction is often priced higher than the strip method because it is more labour-intensive to harvest follicles individually.

The quoted price usually covers the consultation and scalp assessment, the surgical session itself, local anesthesia, and routine post-procedure care and follow-up during your stay. Smaller restorations, such as a modest hairline refinement, sit toward the lower end, while extensive coverage of a large balding area sits toward the upper end and may be planned across more than one session.

Costs not always included in the headline figure can include flights, accommodation for the roughly five-day stay, airport transfers, and any prescribed medication or aftercare products. When you request a quote, ask whether it is based on a confirmed follicle count and whether follow-up is part of the package, so you can compare clinics fairly. KmedTour helps you obtain itemised estimates from accredited Korean clinics before you travel, so the total cost of treatment and travel is clear in advance.
sections.why_korea:
Korea has built a strong reputation in aesthetic and hair-restoration care, supported by a national healthcare framework and a high concentration of clinics experienced in follicular procedures. The Ministry of Health and Welfare oversees medical standards, while the Korea Health Industry Development Institute promotes the country's medical-tourism sector and helps international patients find reputable providers.

Many hospitals and clinics that treat international patients hold accreditation through bodies such as the Korea Institute for Healthcare Accreditation, which assesses patient safety and quality of care. For visitors from Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, this combination of oversight and accreditation offers reassurance that the clinic meets recognised standards.

Practical factors add to the appeal. Clinics that regularly treat overseas patients often provide language support, coordinated scheduling, and clear written quotes, which makes planning a trip more straightforward. Procedures are typically completed within a short stay, so the visit can be arranged around a focused trip. KmedTour works only with accredited Korean clinics and helps you compare options, understand what is included, and prepare for travel, so your decision rests on clear information rather than guesswork.

faqs.q: Will a hair transplant give me permanent results?
faqs.a: Transplanted follicles are taken from donor areas that resist the hormone behind most hair loss, so they generally keep growing in their new position long term. However, a transplant does not stop your remaining native hair from thinning, so further loss elsewhere is possible over the years. Realistic planning during consultation helps set expectations about lasting coverage.

faqs.q: What is the difference between FUE and the strip method?
faqs.a: Follicular unit extraction removes follicles individually, leaving small dot-shaped marks and allowing short haircuts afterward. The strip method, or follicular unit transplantation, removes a thin band of scalp that is divided into grafts and leaves a fine linear mark. Strip surgery can yield many grafts in one session; FUE avoids a line. Your clinic recommends the better fit.

The session is performed under local anesthesia, which numbs the scalp so you do not feel pain while follicles are harvested and placed. You stay awake and can take short breaks during the several hours it takes. Some tightness, mild soreness, and swelling are common in the days afterward and ease with the aftercare your clinic provides.

faqs.q: Why does transplanted hair fall out at first?
faqs.a: It is normal and expected for transplanted hairs to shed within the first two to six weeks. This shock loss is a stage in the process, not a failure. The follicles remain in place beneath the skin, and new hair usually begins to grow after a few months, thickening gradually over roughly a year toward the full result.

faqs.q: How long should I plan to stay in Korea?
faqs.a: A stay of around five days is typical, covering the consultation, the procedure, an early review, and the first wash before you travel home. Flying is generally fine once the clinic confirms the grafts are stable, often near the end of your stay. Follow-up afterward is usually arranged through a return visit or progress photos.
